title: Chasing something that keeps slipping out of reach
phrase: This painting holds the part of a person that keeps returning to a feeling instead of naming it. The warm break and the missing center make the chase visible without turning it into a story. It matches the way they said 'chasing something that keeps slipping out of reach'.

title: Fairness of the Chaos
phrase: Even when life feels messy, the system can still be fair and bring you the calm you want.

title: Calm That Survives the Storm
phrase: The off-center calm block and the pressure band around it show how a person stayed still while the world got loud. The small warm light near the edge suggests the quiet part that refused to disappear.

title: Spring's First Pulse
phrase: This painting holds a person's spring joy in a gentle way, because it starts with the bright first warmth they want to keep close. The drifting blue-green band turns a simple sentence into visible movement, while the small coral cluster keeps the feeling alive and close to the body. It feels like spring without naming it.
